problem_statement: The document addresses how to responsibly oversee LLM-driven large-scale code migration in regulated financial systems, where AI rewrites legacy code and human review struggles to verify functional equivalence and preserve accountability.
magnitude_evidence: "A 2025 industry survey found that over 97% of software companies have adopted AI tools in their engineering processes, with code generation representing the dominant use case (Techreviewer, 2025)."
insufficiency_evidence: "Developers may scan the converted code for obvious errors but lack the time or institutional mandate to perform the deep, line-by-line comparison against the original that would be necessary to catch subtle semantic differences"; standard testing tests converted code in isolation and does not compare behavior against the original across full input range.
solution_mechanisms: mandatory human review checkpoints (pre-migration scoping, mid-migration validation, post-migration certification)
differential testing verifying functional equivalence against original system
